directive: Remove the wrapping transaction from backfillPdIncidents.ts. Let each batch commit independently (auto-commit per batch insert is fine). Accept that rows added to archive.alerts during the ~38-minute run may be missed — this is tolerable given the archive is a low-write historical table and the script is idempotent via the __backfill_legacy_ dedup key. Do not introduce a pg_dump step. Keep all other Round 2 fixes: keyset pagination and the __backfill_legacy_ dedup prefix.
rationale: Two Reviewer rejections, both valid quality concerns. Round 1 (OFFSET pagination + dedup collision) was fixed correctly. Round 2 identifies a legitimate database health hazard: a 38-minute open transaction will block autovacuum on archive.alerts, causing table bloat. The Reviewer offers two remedies; option (a) — drop the transaction — is correct. archive.alerts is a historical archive with rare new writes; snapshot-inconsistency risk during a 38-minute window is negligible. The script is idempotent via the __backfill_legacy_ dedup key (Reviewer-approved in round 2), so any rows missed during the run are safely captured by a re-run. Option (b) — pg_dump snapshot — adds operational steps and complexity for a one-time script with no material benefit over re-run safety. The phase acceptance criteria ('at least 90 days present post-run', 'idempotent') are met by option (a). Non-goals are fully preserved: no DSL modification, no new service, no new secrets.
